perturbation.original_text: Power2Ship may, at any time during the term of this Agreement, assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to one or more of its subsidiaries in which it owns at least 50.1% of the actual equity of the subsidiary on a fully diluted basis and as long as Power2Ship remains the guarantor for the payments and terms and conditions of this Agreement (individually, a "Subsidiary"). Each Subsidiary shall thereafter have the rights and obligations of Power2Ship hereunder with respect to all terms and conditions of this agreement as if such Subsidiary had entered into this Agreement directly with WLI.
perturbation.changed_text: Power2Ship may, at any time during the term of this Agreement, assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to one or more of its subsidiaries, regardless of Power2Ship's ownership stake in the subsidiary, as long as Power2Ship remains the guarantor for the payments and terms and conditions of this Agreement (individually, a "Subsidiary"). Each Subsidiary shall thereafter have the rights and obligations of Power2Ship hereunder with respect to all terms and conditions of this agreement as if such Subsidiary had entered into this Agreement directly with WLI.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the requirements for Power2Ship to assign its rights to a subsidiary. The original text requires at least 50.1% ownership, while the changed text removes this ownership requirement. This contradicts Section 1.6, which states: "WLI may, at any time during the term of this Agreement, assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to any subsidiary in which it owns at least 50.1% of the equity on a fully diluted basis." The Perturbation in 1.4, removes the ownership % and the 50.1% is being referred to in 1.6. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the conditions for valid assignment and the rights and obligations of subsidiaries.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WLI may, at any time during the term of this Agreement, assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to any subsidiary in which it owns at least 50.1% of the equity on a fully diluted basis.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the 50.1% ownership requirement for Power2Ship's subsidiaries, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that specifically requires WLI's subsidiaries to have at least 50.1% ownership. This creates a direct inconsistency in the contract regarding ownership requirements for subsidiary assignments, leading to potential disputes and uncertainty, therefore making it a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to Section 3.6 below the price of the Product, excluding the Licensed Programs, is $[*] per Unit. The total price for both of the Licensed Programs together is $[*] per month per Product ("License Fee"). The License Fee shall begin on the date the Product is shipped to a customer and shall be paid by Power2Ship to WLI by the 7th of each month for the prior month (prorated if the Unit was shipped during the prior month) and shall continue for as long as the Product is active on any wireless network. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Power2Ship is obligated to pay the License Fee to WLI for a minimum of 36 months.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to Section 3.6 below the price of the Product, excluding the Licensed Programs, is $[*] per Unit. The total price for both of the Licensed Programs together is $[*] per month per Product ("License Fee"). The License Fee shall begin on the date the Product is shipped to a customer and shall be paid by Power2Ship to WLI by the 7th of each month for the prior month (prorated if the Unit was shipped during the prior month) and shall continue for as long as the Product is active on any wireless network. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Power2Ship is obligated to pay the License Fee to WLI for a minimum of 12 months.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the minimum duration for which Power2Ship is obligated to pay the License Fee. The original text states 36 months, while the changed text reduces this to 12 months. This contradicts section 3.3, which references deactivation and reactivation of units after a 36-month period: "Once Power2Ship has paid WLI the License Fee for a minimum of 36 months for a particular Unit, Power2Ship may discontinue paying the month License Fee only if that particular Unit has been deactivated from the wireless network.". The perturbation changes 3.1, and is referencing the 36 months again at 3.3. This contradiction introduces uncertainty regarding the actual minimum payment duration and affects the financial obligations of Power2Ship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Once Power2Ship has paid WLI the License Fee for a minimum of 36 months for a particular Unit, Power2Ship may discontinue paying the month License Fee only if that particular Unit has been deactivated from the wireless network. If a Unit is deactivated after 36 months and subsequently is reactivated, Power2Ship must restart the payment of the License Fee for as long as that particular Unit remains active on the wireless network.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the minimum duration of the License Fee obligation from 36 months to 12 months. The contradicted text explicitly states that Power2Ship may discontinue paying the License Fee only if the Unit has been deactivated after a *minimum* of 36 months of payments. By changing the initial obligation to 12 months, the changed text directly contradicts the 36-month requirement in the contradicted section, creating an inconsistency regarding when Power2Ship can discontinue payments.

perturbation.original_text: Monthly License Fees must be paid no later then the 7th of the month for all Units active during the previous month. For example, on the 7th of July Power2Ship will pay for Units that were active in June.
perturbation.changed_text: Monthly License Fees must be paid no later then the 15th of the month for all Units active during the previous month. For example, on the 15th of July Power2Ship will pay for Units that were active in June.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the payment deadline for the Monthly License Fees. The original text specifies the 7th of the month, while the changed text sets the deadline to the 15th. This contradicts Section 3.1, which states: "The License Fee shall begin on the date the Product is shipped to a customer and shall be paid by Power2Ship to WLI by the 7th of each month for the prior month". Perturbation is changing the 7th day payment at 7.3, which the 7th day is mentioned again at 3.1. This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the actual payment deadline, potentially leading to disputes over late payment penalties and the calculation of interest on overdue amounts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Section 3.6 below the price of the Product, excluding the Licensed Programs, is $[*] per Unit. The total price for both of the Licensed Programs together is $[*] per month per Product ("License Fee"). The License Fee shall begin on the date the Product is shipped to a customer and shall be paid by Power2Ship to WLI by the 7th of each month for the prior month (prorated if the Unit was shipped during the prior month) and shall continue for as long as the Product is active on any wireless network. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Power2Ship is obligated to pay the License Fee to WLI for a minimum of 36 months.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline for Monthly License Fees from the 7th to the 15th of the month. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, Section 3.1, which states that the License Fee 'shall be paid by Power2Ship to WLI by the 7th of each month for the prior month.' This is a clear inconsistency regarding a critical term (payment deadline), leading to potential disputes and uncertainty, hence it's a strong contradiction.
